title: AWS Study Group Workshop - AI in Development
content:
Event Information Event Name: AWS Study Group Workshop - AI in Development Lifecycle
Date & Time: May 9, 2026
Location: 26th Floor, Bitexco Tower, 02 Hai Trieu Street, Saigon Ward, Ho Chi Minh City
Role: Attendee
Event Objectives The event focused on several key objectives:
Workshop Organization: Sharing experiences and introducing how to organize effective AWS workshops AWS Technologies: Providing guidance on AWS technologies and best practices AI in Development: Demonstrating how AI is supporting nearly the entire programming and application development lifecycle Speakers The event featured presentations from experienced AWS community members:
Anh Long (Admin)
Topic: “How to Get Addicted to Learning Like Social Media” Shared strategies for building sustainable learning habits Anh Thịnh (Admin)
Topic: “Automated Prompt Engineering: Enhancing LLM Output Quality” Explored techniques for improving AI-generated content Anh Khang (Admin)
Topic: “Why You Haven’t Started Working Yet” Discussed career development and job readiness Female Speaker (Software Developer)
Topic: “BMAD” (Build, Measure, Analyze, Deploy) Covered modern development methodologies Key Takeaways 1. Learning Habits and Motivation Learned strategies to make learning as engaging as social media Understood the importance of consistent learning habits Discovered techniques to maintain motivation in technical learning 2. AI-Powered Development Gained insights into how AI tools support the entire development lifecycle Learned about automated prompt engineering techniques Understood how to leverage LLMs effectively in development work 3. Career Development Received guidance on preparing for professional work Understood the gap between learning and job readiness Learned about industry expectations and requirements 4. Modern Development Practices Explored the BMAD methodology Understood the importance of measurement and analysis in development Learned about continuous deployment practices Personal Reflections This event was incredibly valuable for my internship journey. The speakers provided practical insights that directly apply to my current learning path with AWS. The session on AI in development was particularly relevant, as it showed me how the tools I’m learning to use (like Kiro) fit into the broader landscape of AI-assisted development.
The workshop organization tips will be useful if I ever need to share knowledge with others or conduct training sessions. Most importantly, the career development session helped me understand what skills and mindset I need to develop beyond technical knowledge.
Group photo with fellow attendees at the AWS Study Group Workshop
Networking Opportunities Connected with AWS Study Group admins and community members Met fellow interns and learners in the AWS ecosystem Exchanged contact information for future collaboration Joined discussions about ongoing projects and learning paths Action Items Based on the event, I plan to:
Apply the learning habit strategies to my daily study routine Experiment with prompt engineering techniques in my work with AI tools Focus on building practical skills alongside theoretical knowledge Explore the BMAD methodology in my future projects Stay connected with the AWS Study Group community Resources AWS Study Group Facebook Discord Lamentum Event slides and materials (shared via Discord) Overall Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
This event significantly enhanced my understanding of both technical and professional development in the AWS ecosystem.

title: Internship Report
content:
Internship Report Student Information: Full Name: Nguyễn Tấn Lộc
Phone Number: 0965438184
Email: locnguyetan0210@gmail.com
University: Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology (HUTECH)
Major: Software Engineering
Class: 22DTHD7
Internship Company: Amazon Web Services Vietnam Co., Ltd.
Internship Position: FCJ Cloud Intern
Internship Duration: From April 17, 2026 to July 10, 2026
Report Content Worklog Proposal Events Participated Self-evaluation

title: Week 1 Worklog

title: Self-evaluation
content:
Throughout my internship at [Company Name] from [Start Date] to [End Date], I had the opportunity to learn, practice, and apply the knowledge I gained at university to a real work environment.
To objectively reflect on my internship process, I would like to evaluate myself based on the following criteria:
No. Criteria Description Excellent Good Average 1 Professional Knowledge & Skills Understanding of the field, applying knowledge in practice, tool usage skills, work quality ☐ ☐ ☐ 2 Learning Ability Absorbing new knowledge, learning quickly ☐ ☐ ☐ 3 Proactiveness Self-learning, taking on tasks without waiting for instructions ☐ ☐ ☐ 4 Sense of Responsibility Completing work on time, ensuring quality ☐ ☐ ☐ 5 Discipline Following schedules, regulations, work processes ☐ ☐ ☐ 6 Growth Mindset Willing to receive feedback and improve oneself ☐ ☐ ☐ 7 Communication Presenting ideas, reporting work clearly ☐ ☐ ☐ 8 Teamwork Working effectively with colleagues, participating in teams ☐ ☐ ☐ 9 Professional Conduct Respecting colleagues, partners, work environment ☐ ☐ ☐ 10 Problem-Solving Thinking Identifying problems, proposing solutions, creativity ☐ ☐ ☐ 11 Contribution to Project/Organization Work effectiveness, improvement initiatives, team recognition ☐ ☐ ☐ 12 Overall Overall assessment of the entire internship process ☐ ☐ ☐ Areas for Improvement [Add areas you need to improve]
